title: Bar Chart: The chart displays information about fitness membership numbers across different a...
description: The chart displays information about fitness membership numbers across different age groups in an Asian country from 1970 to 2000. Summarize the key information by highlighting the main features and make appropriate comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Emigration from the UK by age group (1970–2000)

1. Hiểu đề bài

Biểu đồ cho thấy số người rời khỏi UK, chia theo 3 nhóm tuổi: Under 18; 18–35; 36–65, qua các năm từ 1970 đến 2000

Dạng này: xu hướng theo thời gian \+ so sánh giữa các nhóm tuổi nha

2. Cách viết Introduction

Paraphrase với:

  • the number of people emigrating
  • three age groups
  • over a 30-year period

3. Cách viết Overview

  • Nhóm 18–35 luôn cao nhất trong hầu hết các năm
  • Nhóm 36–65 thấp nhất nhưng tăng dần theo thời gian
  • Under 18 biến động nhưng xu hướng chung là tăng
  • Đỉnh điểm rơi vào khoảng giữa–cuối giai đoạn (đặc biệt 1995)

4. Cách chia Body

BODY 1 – Nhóm 18–35 (nổi bật nhất)

  • 1970: \~2,100 → tăng mạnh lên 4,000 (1975)
  • Giảm nhẹ 1980–1985 (\~2,500 → \~1,700)
  • Tăng lại và đạt đỉnh \~5,000 (1995), sau đó giảm còn \~4,000 (2000)

→ biến động mạnh nhưng luôn cao nhất

BODY 2 – Under 18 & 36–65

  • Under 18: 1,000 → 2,000 → giảm nhẹ → tăng lại lên \~3,600 (2000)
  • 36–65: 500 → tăng đều lên \~2,800 (2000), không có giảm đáng kể

→ nhóm lớn tuổi tăng ổn định; nhóm trẻ biến động

5. Gợi ý paraphrase

  • the number of emigrants
  • rose sharply / fell slightly
  • fluctuated over time
  • reached a peak
  • remained the highest / lowest

Ví dụ:

The number of people aged 18–35 peaked at around 5,000 in 1995.

The bar chart illustrates the number of people emigrating from the UK across three age groups between 1970 and 2000.

Overall, individuals aged 18–35 consistently accounted for the largest proportion of emigrants, while those aged 36–65 were the smallest group. All age groups experienced fluctuations over time, but there was a general upward trend towards the end of the period.

In 1970, around 2,100 people aged 18–35 emigrated, compared to 1,000 under-18s and approximately 500 individuals aged 36–65. By 1975, the 18–35 group rose sharply to 4,000, while the other groups also increased to 2,000 and 700, respectively.

After a slight decline in 1980, emigration patterns became more varied. The number of under-18s peaked at around 2,800 in 1985 before falling slightly. In contrast, the 18–35 group dipped to roughly 1,700 in 1985 but later surged to a high of 5,000 in 1995, before declining to 4,000 in 2000.

The 36–65 group showed a steady upward trend overall, rising from 500 in 1970 to about 2,800 by 2000, despite minor fluctuations.
